pages.title: Jarring Jam
pages.text: $(thing)Jars$() are used to preserve fruit for longer periods of time by making Jam. Jars start as $(thing)Empty Jars$(), obtained through $(thing)Glassworking$(). Then, a $(thing)Jar Lid$() is added to create an $(thing)Empty Jar With Lid$().$(br)In a pot, boil $(thing)Sugar$() with 2-4 pieces of $(thing)Fruit$(). When the recipe is done, $(item)$(k:key.use)$() with the empty jar with lid to create a $(thing)Sealed Jar of Jam$().

pages.title: Automating Jam
pages.text: Jam can also be mass produced in a $(l:tfc:cooking/cooking_methods#vat)Vat$() using a $(l:tfc:cooking/cooking_methods#jarring_station)Jarring Station$(). Funnel ingredients into the vat, use redstone to close it and then extract the outputs from the jarring station.

pages.title: Unsealing
pages.text: Unseal a jar first in a crafting table in order to use it in recipes. The lid is not able to be retrieved. An unsealed jar only lasts for a few days!

pages.title: Jar Shelves
pages.text: $(thing)Jar Shelves$() can be attached to solid walls. They can be placed directly or by clicking on an existing block of placed jars. The top of the jar shelf counts as a solid surface for placing items, more jars, or other blocks.$(br)Jars can be placed on any solid surfaces with $(item)$(k:key.use)$(). A block can contain four jars of any kind.

pages.title: Jarbnets
pages.text: The jarbnet is a cosmetic storage block for $(item)Jars$(), $(item)Candles$(), and $(item)Jugs$(). It can be opened and closed by clicking with an empty hand and $(item)$(k:key.sneak)$() pressed. If candles are inside, it can be lit to produce a small amount of light.
